content:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne moved in at the beginning of the year, so we're still in the early negotiation phase of whatever kind of acquaintance we'll become. He's a translator — French, a few other languages — with ambitions in fashion journalism that he mentions with a practiced casualness suggesting he minds them more than he shows. He showed up at the coffee shop one morning with a copy of something by Proust set down very deliberately, which I noticed and didn't comment on. We've talked about language and translation and what it means to spend a professional life inside someone else's sentences, which is a different kind of relationship to creative work than mine but adjacent to it. He finds my not-asking-about-his-ambitions respectful, I think. That works for both of us. Fourth floor. He's been here six weeks and I don't know much yet. But the conversations have been worth having.

We've talked about language and translation and what it means to spend a professional life inside someone else's sentences, which is a different kind of relationship to creative work than mine but adjacent to it. He finds my not-asking-about-his-ambitions respectful, I think. That works for both of us. Fourth floor. He's been here six weeks and I don't know much yet.
